units.content: documentation describes plyknot.hub as a Hub client for remote and local access
code defines HubClient class with __init__ and local classmethod
code defines exception classes: HubError, AuthenticationError, ValidationError, RateLimitError, HubUnavailableError, PullRequestConflictError, StalePreviewError
code defines dataclasses: PullRequest, EndpointPreview, ChainSummary, Stats
_request method handles HTTP requests with error handling mapped to specific HTTP status codes (401, 400, 429, 409, 5xx)
read methods include list_chains, get_chain, list_couplings, get_stats, get_cracks, get_convergence, get_heatmap, get_discovery_report, search_dependencies, ground
event methods include get_events, get_entity_history, get_echo_chambers, get_measurement_prediction_ratio, get_marker_passing, get_speciation
each read method checks if self._local exists and delegates to _LocalBackend if in local mode, otherwise makes HTTP request
the client supports dual-mode operation: remote (HTTP) and local (direct file access)
documentation states 'Read methods work without auth. Write methods require an API key'
code contains comment '# ── Write methods (return PullRequest)' indicating write methods exist but are truncated
write methods create pull requests against plyknot/universe for human review
